title: Encasing LAMP mixture in paraffin
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e0gvl","text":"This protocol can be used to encase LAMP mastermix and primers in paraffin.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a 10X primermix according to the following scheme %s %s
Add the following components to a micro centrifuge tube %s %s

Open the centrifuge tube and let dry at 37 C until only solid is left.Note: The longer this step takes, the more activity the enzyme loses. It is recommended to dry in a storage area with a low humidity, as to speed up this step. To minimize the loss of activity, lyophilisation is recommended.
Heat at 60 C for 5 minutes to let the wax melt and encapsulate the dried LAMP mixture
After the wax dries, the continued activity of the enzyme can be tested. To do this, add the following components %s %s
Record the color of all samples. Yellowing of a sample indicates a positive result.
In case of doubt regarding the result of a sample, run it on an agarose gel. The presence of many bands, often not individually recognizable, indicates a positive result.
creator: Niels Appelman
